name: Edifier W820NB Plus Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Headphones

specs.Special Features: Hi-Res Audio certified, foldable, app EQ

whatWorks: Hybrid ANC combined with LDAC Hi-Res Audio at $79 CAD is a combination that costs $200-$300 more on competing brands — feedforward + feedback microphone placement delivers better noise cancellation than feedforward-only designs at the budget tier.
49h battery without ANC means these can last an entire workweek on a single charge. 38h with ANC on still outlasts most premium headphones in terms of continuous playback time.
Multipoint Bluetooth allows two devices simultaneously — a feature that distinguishes these from many competitors at the same price who offer single-device connection only.
Foldable design with USB-C charging means these are practical for travel — the fold is compact enough for a bag side pocket without requiring a dedicated case.
worthKnowing: ANC performance doesn't match Sony WH-1000XM6 or Bose QC45 — this is expected at the price, but buyers upgrading from premium headphones will notice the gap in loud environments like transit or open-plan offices.
LDAC requires a compatible Android device and source with LDAC enabled — iPhone users get AAC only, and the Hi-Res Audio certification isn't relevant on Apple devices.
Earcup cushioning is adequate but not plush — the memory foam is thinner than premium competitors and may compress and thin further with extended use over months.

The Edifier Connect app offers EQ customization but the interface is basic compared to Jabra Sound+, Sony Headphones Connect, or JBL Headphones — limited preset options and no personalization testing.

commonComplaints.title: ANC doesn't block much on the…
commonComplaints.body: ANC doesn't block much on the subway — takes the edge off but you still hear everything. Good for offices, not for transit.

commonComplaints.title: LDAC is Android only which I…
commonComplaints.body: LDAC is Android only which I knew going in but it's still disappointing that iPhone users are second-class citizens on these.

commonComplaints.title: Cushion thinned out after six months…
commonComplaints.body: Cushion thinned out after six months of daily use — still comfortable but noticeably less plush than when I bought them.

commonComplaints.title: App is very basic compared to…
commonComplaints.body: App is very basic compared to Sony or Jabra — two EQ presets and a few ANC switches. Not much to work with.

verdict: The Edifier W820NB Plus competes directly with the Anker Soundcore Q45 for budget ANC over-ear supremacy and wins on ANC technology (hybrid vs single-sided) while matching it on LDAC and battery life. At $79 CAD, the combination of Hybrid ANC and LDAC is remarkable value. Cushion durability and app depth are the main trade-offs versus spending more. For Android users who want the best ANC performance in the sub-$100 category, these edge ahead of the competition.

realBuyers.quote: LDAC and hybrid ANC at $79 — I genuinely can't believe this exists. The sound quality is incredible for the price.
Office background noise disappears completely with ANC on — I forget there are 30 people around me.
49 hours is a real number — I've gone almost two weeks without thinking about charging.
Best budget ANC headphones period. Not perfect but nothing at $79 comes close to this feature set.

sources.title: Edifier W820NB Plus Review — Android Authority

metaDescription: Edifier W820NB Plus review for Canada: Hybrid ANC, LDAC Hi-Res Audio, 49h battery, multipoint at $79. Honest on ANC limits vs premium and cushion durability. ~$79 CAD.
introText: The Edifier W820NB Plus are budget over-ear headphones featuring hybrid active noise cancellation and LDAC Hi-Res Audio — a premium feature combination at $79 CAD that outpunches its price tier significantly. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B09Q1LV8YD) at around $79 CAD.
